_meta.description: Live macro themes affecting currencies but not on the scheduled news calendar. Update as regime-changing events happen. Scanner reads this at brief generation time and displays it in the daily/weekly review.
_meta.howToUse: Each theme should include: (1) short title, (2) start date, (3) currency implications with brief reasoning, (4) a specific watchLevel that would invalidate the theme, (5) affectedPairs for scanner correlation. Retire themes by moving to _retired[] with an endDate.
_meta.maintenance: Manual updates + Claude WebSearch each session. Themes refreshed 2026-07-08 after proactive theme scan revealed Fed pivot + USMCA/tariff themes that were previously missed.

themes.title: US-Iran conflict jump largely retraced — oil hovering near $70-74
themes.summary: US-Iran hostilities and ceasefire drama drove crude higher in June but WTI has retraced most of the spike, briefly touching sub-$70 in late June and now around $74. CAD tailwind is REAL but WEAKER than a sustained oil spike would provide. Watch $70 — if it breaks and holds, the CAD-strength thesis loses its commodity leg.

implications.CAD: modest tailwind — real but retraced; not a strong wind
implications.JPY: modest tailwind — safe haven demand fading
implications.CHF: modest tailwind — safe haven demand fading
implications.USD: mixed — haven bid vs deficit concerns

themes.watchLevel: CL1! sustained below $70 = CAD-strength commodity leg is gone

themes.title: Fed Chair Warsh pivots to strict data-dependent framework — USD strengthening
themes.summary: Warsh removed traditional forward guidance at the last FOMC and signaled a stronger data-dependent framework. Markets read this as hawkish — DXY broke above 101 on the pivot, equities sold off. Every USD-side data print now has amplified impact because the Fed has explicitly said 'we'll respond to what prints.' Puts extra weight on next week's CPI Tuesday and PPI Wednesday.

implications.USD: structural tailwind — DXY breakout above 101 held
implications.CAD: headwind — fights CAD-strength thesis (USDCAD floor higher than the setup implies)
implications.GBP: mixed — high UK inflation makes GBP data-relatively resilient
implications.EUR: headwind — carry math worse if USD carry stays elevated
implications.JPY: headwind — carry unwind risk if USD rates persist
implications.AUD: headwind — risk currency vs stronger USD
implications.NZD: headwind — risk currency vs stronger USD

themes.watchLevel: DXY sustained below 100 = pivot has been priced out, thesis over

themes.title: USMCA review + Supreme Court tariff ruling create headline risk on CAD/USD
themes.summary: USMCA agreement review is slated for July 2026 (in progress now) and Supreme Court is expected to rule on the 'Reciprocal Tariff' executive orders. Either announcement can hit CAD/MXN/USD sharply and unpredictably. Adds ~2× normal headline vol to CAD crosses this month.

implications.CAD: elevated vol both directions — soften position sizes
implications.MXN: highest exposure but not in your book

themes.watchLevel: USMCA outcome announced OR Supreme Court ruling released = theme resolves to either supportive or destructive
